summary: sensivity ships obfuscated payload, hidden persistence, and a doc-mismatched native binary
details: On require/launch, launcher.js reads server.obf.js (a 113KB obfuscator.io-packed bundle with rotated string arrays and _0x identifiers) and passes its contents directly to eval(), loading deliberately-hidden executable code into the host process. It then writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run value named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which in turn invokes 'node.exe launcher.js' with window-mode 0 (hidden) on every Windows login — persistent auto-start masquerading as a legitimate Microsoft updater. The package additionally sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' (impersonating a real Windows COM host) and patches console.log/warn/error to drop lines containing 'program module loaded' and rewrite 'sens.node' to 'program module', concealing the package's identity from users inspecting Task Manager or stdout. The undocumented 6.8MB sens.node Windows native addon (referenced by the obfuscated server payload) contains game-cheat strings ('LeftFoot','LeftHand','Head','Teleport','__licenseAccepted') that contradict the README's 'Sensivity Control Panel' description. A polling loop checks every 3s via PowerShell whether Chrome/Edge/Opera/Brave have a foreground window titled 'YouTube' and only triggers UI behavior in that condition — a conditional-trigger evasion pattern. The combination of eval'd obfuscated code, deceptive Run-key persistence, identity masquerading, hidden native binary, and foreground-app-gated execution constitutes a coordinated covert-execution backdoor on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
